text: Laser ablation treatment is a standard treatment for high-grade EIN, and in other words, at the Homerton Anyal Neoplasia Service. It is performed with high-resolution anoscopy visualization, which consists of a proctoscope, five percent acetic acid, and a corposcope. Patient is always placed in a lithotomy position.

The surgeon inserts a proctoscope and applies acetic acid to highlight the squamous-columnar junction, making abnormal areas appear whiter. Two lesions are identified at one and seven o'clock positions, which can be treated with a diode laser.

The surgeon visually identifies the high-grade disease area, demarcates it for treatment, and begins laser ablation. The device used includes a suction element to aspirate the laser plume during the procedure.

The surgeon performs laser ablation with minimal lateral thermal spread, taking approximately thirty minutes per quadrant. The anoscopist maintains a comfortable position throughout the procedure. Minor bleeding is observed and controlled with topical agents.
patients can go home the same day. The pain can be an issue, but scarring is rare. The epithelium will resurface in one to two months.
